ChooseAlgo.Title: Initial Setup
ChooseAlgo.Description: Please choose your graybyte database format. You cannot switch between them later on. The only way is to delete the entire database and start from scratch. Both use visual similarity frame check as default algorithm. In both cases you can find duplicates even if different resolution, added watermark, changes to fps / bitrate etc
ChooseAlgo.Option16Details:
• Fastest
• More false positives
• pHash algorithm is NOT available
ChooseAlgo.Option32Details:
• Slower than 16x16 (theoretically 4x slower but practically in most cases +40-60% additional scan time)
• Less false positives
• pHash algorithm is available. More robust when duplicates differ due to changes in contrast, brightness, scaling, or minor edits (but not automatically better, it just works differently)
• When a video is first added to the database, pHash signature is automatically generated, making the initial scan slower. On subsequent rescans, you can switch between both algorithms with negligible performance cost, enabling highly efficient duplicate detection
ChooseAlgo.FoldersHint: Optional. You can add folders now or skip and do it later in Settings.
CustomSelection.Title: Custom Selection
CustomSelection.IgnoreGroupsWithCheckedI: Ignore groups which contain checked items
While active, any group which contains at least one checked item will be ignored
CustomSelection.WildcardRequired: Please add a wildcard (*) at the end of the path so partial matches work.
CustomSelection.WildcardNote: Note: Supports the following wildcards: '*' and '?'. The backslash character '\' escapes.

DatabaseViewer.RemoveNote: Removing an entry never touches the file on disk.

Dialog.AddPatternPrompt: Enter a wildcard pattern to exclude matching directory names

Preset.Label: Preset:
MainWindow.ContextMenu.DeleteFromDiskRec: Delete from disk (to recycle bin if possible)
MainWindow.ContextMenu.MarkNotMatchToolt: Hides this group and exclude it from future scans, except when there is a new duplicate.
MainWindow.ContextMenu.RemoveFromListAnd: Remove from list only and exclude from future scans
Remove from list only + create symbol links
MainWindow.ContextMenu.RemoveFromListSym: Remove from list only + create symbol links + exclude
MainWindow.ContextMenu.SymbolLinkTooltip: Links all selected items to the first unselected item in each group.
MainWindow.ContextMenu.RemoveFromListAnd: Remove from list only + create hard links
MainWindow.ContextMenu.RemoveFromListHar: Remove from list only + create hard links + exclude
MainWindow.ContextMenu.HardLinkTooltip: Replaces each checked file with a hard link to the first unchecked item of its group. Both files must be on the same volume.
MainWindow.EmptyState.Subtitle: Click “Scan” in the toolbar to begin.
MainWindow.EmptyState.Title: Please start your first scan
MainWindow.Filter.PathNote: Note: if only one item of a group is matching, all group items will be shown
MainWindow.Selection.CheckMissingFiles: Check Missing Files (No Longer on Disk)
MainWindow.Settings.AlwaysRetryFailedSam: Files whose frame extraction failed once are normally skipped on every later scan. On = retry them every scan — useful after repairing or replacing broken files, at the cost of re-attempting genuinely broken ones each time. (Audio fingerprint failures are tracked separately.)
MainWindow.Settings.AutoBackupScanResult: Automatically save the results list to backup.scanresults (next to the database) whenever it changes. A crash or forced close mid-triage restores your progress on the next launch; the backup is removed after a clean save-and-exit.
MainWindow.Settings.CompareHorizontallyF: Additionally compare every pair against a horizontally mirrored version, catching mirror-image re-uploads (marked as flipped in the results). Cost: roughly doubles the comparison work.
MainWindow.Settings.CustomCommandsPlaceh: E.g.: 'nemo --tabs %f', where '%f' is the placeholder for the file(s)
MainWindow.Settings.CustomCommandsToolti: Enter your own commands to open single or multiple files.
MainWindow.Settings.CustomDatabaseFolder: Specify a custom database folder. Otherwise it uses the current VDF folder.
MainWindow.Settings.DatabaseCheckpointIn: Periodically save the scan database during hashing to prevent data loss on crash. Set to 0 to disable.
MainWindow.Settings.CustomFFArgumentsToo: FF(mpeg/probe) arguments passed to the end of the internal arguments. Most users can leave this empty.
MainWindow.Settings.DragDropHint: Hint: You can also add folders via drag & drop
MainWindow.Settings.SearchDirectoriesHin: Folders where VDF looks for media files. All files found here are added to the scan database.
MainWindow.Settings.ExcludeDirectoriesHi: Folders to skip completely — they are never scanned and their files never enter the database. Use this for folders you always want to ignore (e.g. backups, system folders).
MainWindow.Settings.DurationDifferenceTo: Pre-filter: two videos are frame-compared at all only when their durations differ by less than this percentage (of each video's own length; the smaller allowance wins). Example: at 20%, a 100s and a 130s clip are never compared. Set 0 to disable the percent rule and use only the seconds bounds below.
MainWindow.Settings.DurationDifferenceMi: Floor (seconds) for the percent rule above: short clips always get at least this much allowance. Example: at 20% two 10-second clips would only tolerate 2s; with a floor of 5 they still compare when within 5s. 0 = no floor. If the percent rule is 0, this acts as a flat seconds tolerance.
MainWindow.Settings.DurationDifferenceMa: Cap (seconds) for the percent rule above: long videos never get more than this allowance. Example: at 20% a 2-hour movie would tolerate 24 minutes of difference — capping at 30s stops unrelated long films of similar length from flooding the candidate list. 0 = no cap.
MainWindow.Settings.MaxSamplingDurationS: Sample all comparison frames within the first N seconds of each video instead of across the full length. Versions of the same content with different tails (extra credits, appended ads) then sample aligned positions and still match. Example: 600 compares every video by its first 10 minutes. 0 = spread over the full duration.
MainWindow.Settings.ExcludeHardLinksTool: When two matched files are hard links to the SAME data on disk, do not report them as duplicates — deleting one would free no space. Checked only after a visual match, by comparing volume and file IDs. Useful on libraries already deduplicated with hard links.
MainWindow.Settings.ExcludeReparsePoints: Skip NTFS reparse points (symbolic links, junctions, cloud placeholders) while scanning folders. Effect: the same physical file is not scanned twice through a link, and cloud-only placeholder files are not forcibly downloaded.
MainWindow.Settings.ExtendedFFToolsLoggi: If activated ffmpeg and ffprobe errors will be written to the log
MainWindow.Settings.FilesNote: These filters control which files from the database are included in the duplicate comparison. Files that don't match are skipped during comparison but remain in the database — changing these filters and rescanning will re-evaluate them.
MainWindow.Settings.GeneratePreviewImage: Generate preview thumbnails for the results list when the scan finishes. Off = scans finish faster and use less memory, but result rows have no preview image — use the comparer window to eyeball matches.
MainWindow.Settings.ThumbnailMaxWidthToo: Width of the preview thumbnails shown in the results list, in pixels. Display only — never affects which files match. Higher = sharper but uses more memory. Takes effect on the next scan.
MainWindow.Settings.IgnoreBlackPixelsToo: Exclude black pixels from the frame comparison, so letterboxed or pillarboxed copies (black bars) match their bar-less originals — the uniform bars no longer dominate the similarity score. In rare cases this can also cause false positives. Slightly slower comparison.
MainWindow.Settings.IgnoreWhitePixelsToo: Exclude white pixels from the frame comparison, so files with white borders or backgrounds (document scans, product shots) match despite differing borders. In rare cases this can also cause false positives. Slightly slower comparison.
MainWindow.Settings.LogExcludedFilesTool: Log when files are excluded from the scan and why (limited to avoid log spam).
MainWindow.Settings.MaxDegreeOfParalleli: How many files are processed at once, shared across all fast drives (SSDs). -1 = all CPU cores (recommended); 1 = strictly one file at a time (lowest system impact). Spinning hard disks are additionally capped per drive by the setting below.
MainWindow.Settings.MiscHint: Hint: Mouse hover options to get more information about them
MainWindow.Settings.MiscWikiHint: Hint: For best settings please check out our
MainWindow.Settings.PercentTooltip: Similarity threshold for the visual (frame) comparison — how alike the sampled frames of two files must be to count as duplicates. Higher = stricter and fewer matches. Example: 100% finds only near-identical frames; ~95% catches typical re-encodes; 85% also surfaces watermarked or slightly cropped copies at the cost of more false positives.
MainWindow.Settings.RestartHint: ¹ Restart VDF to apply changes
MainWindow.Settings.SaveResultsOnExitToo: Ask whether to save the results list when closing the app while duplicates are still listed. Off = closing silently discards the in-memory list (the auto-backup option may still hold a copy).
MainWindow.Settings.AutoApplySelectionPr: After every scan, automatically run the chosen custom-selection preset (for example: check everything except the highest resolution). Results arrive pre-checked according to your rule, ready for review and deletion. Create presets in the selection menu first.
Log.AutoApplyPresetMissing: Selection preset '{0}' configured for auto-apply was not found
MainWindow.Settings.SaveTooltip: Note: Settings are automatically saved on normal exit
MainWindow.Settings.ScanAgainstEntireDat: Compare the scanned folders against EVERY file the database has ever seen, not just the current include list. Finds duplicates of files stored on other drives or previously scanned locations. WARNING: the gather stage then processes the entire database — the folder list no longer limits how much work a scan does.
MainWindow.Settings.OnlySameFolderDuplic:
Controls how folder relationships affect duplicate detection. Depth applies to both non-None modes (1 = immediate parent folder, 2 = folder + parent, etc.).
- None: no folder restriction
- SameFolderOnly: only flag duplicates within the same folder
- DifferentFolderOnly: only flag duplicates across different folders
MainWindow.Settings.ScanAgainstNonExisti: If checked non-existing files which have all necessary information are included
MainWindow.Settings.ThumbnailsTooltip: Number of frames sampled per video for the visual comparison, spread evenly over the timeline (1 = midpoint only, 3 = 25/50/75%). More frames = fewer false matches, but slower scanning and a bigger database. Changing this invalidates the cached frame data — the next scan re-extracts every video.
MainWindow.Settings.UseExifCreationDate: Use EXIF Creation Date for Images
MainWindow.Settings.UseExifCreationDateT: For photos, use the EXIF date taken (DateTimeOriginal) instead of the file-system creation date. Date-based sorting and best-pick decisions then reflect when the photo was shot, not when the file was copied. Videos are unaffected.
MainWindow.Settings.UseNativeFfmpegBindi: If activated it will call ffmpeg functions natively instead of spawning a new ffmpeg.exe process. This is MUCH(!!) faster. WARNING: You must use the latest SHARED and STABLE ffmpeg version. If something goes wrong then whole VDF will crash.
MainWindow.Settings.UsePHashing: Use pHash instead of visual similarity
MainWindow.Settings.UsePHashingTooltip: Compare videos with 64-bit perceptual hashes instead of raw 32x32 grayscale frames: much faster comparison on large libraries and robust to small brightness shifts, slightly coarser than the grayscale diff. Images always use the grayscale method regardless. Ignored if you're using an old database with 16x16 graybyte values.
MainWindow.Settings.WildcardNote: Wildcards: * matches any characters, ? matches a single character. Examples: *.tmp — all .tmp files, thumb_* — files starting with 'thumb_'. Use \\ (Windows) or \ (Linux/macOS) to escape special characters.
MainWindow.Stats.PotentialSavingsTooltip: Space freed if only the largest file of every group were kept
MainWindow.Tooltip.CleanDatabase: Removes entries which file no longer exists or which failed on ffmpeg/ffprobe
MainWindow.Tooltip.Rescan: Re-run the scan but without enumerating and gathering file informations. Changing certain settings will only take effect during a full scan.

Message.NativeFfmpegLibrariesMissing:
The native FFmpeg binding is enabled, but the FFmpeg shared libraries were not found.

Your FFmpeg/FFprobe executables were found, but the native binding cannot use them — it needs the matching shared-library build ({0}, {1}):
  {2}

You have two options:
  • Disable the native FFmpeg binding in Settings to use your existing FFmpeg executable (process mode). This is the quickest fix.
  • Or install the {0} shared libraries so the native binding can find them (next to the VDF executable, in a 'bin' subfolder, or on the library search path).

MainWindow.Settings.Test.Description: Pick two files you believe are duplicates and run the test. They are compared with your current settings (save is not required) and the report explains at which step the pair passes or fails detection.
MainWindow.Settings.Test.SelectFile: Select a video or image file
MainWindow.Settings.Diagnostics.Descript: Collect info for a bug report. 'Copy diagnostics' puts your ffmpeg version, native-binding status and the settings that affect matching on the clipboard. 'Export graybytes' writes the database's 32×32 grayscale hashes to a file with NO file paths or names — safe to attach to an issue.
MainWindow.Settings.Diagnostics.ExportGr: Failed to export graybytes diagnostic. See the log for details.
MainWindow.Settings.EnablePartialClipDet: Second comparison stage that finds PARTIAL duplicates by audio: clips cut out of longer videos, or the same content with different endings. Requires an audio fingerprint of every file — the fingerprint pass decodes each file's full audio track once (slow on first scan, cached afterwards).
MainWindow.Settings.PartialClipDetection: Warning: Enabling this will significantly increase scan duration. The full audio of every video must be decoded for fingerprinting. Results are cached, so subsequent scans will be faster.
MainWindow.Settings.PartialClipMinRatioT: A shorter file is tested as a clip of a longer one only when its duration is at least this percentage of the longer file. Raise it to skip absurdly short snippets, lower it to catch tiny excerpts. Example: at 55%, a 10-minute clip is never tested against a 2-hour source.
MainWindow.Settings.PartialClipSimilarit: Minimum audio-fingerprint similarity for a clip match, evaluated at the best-aligned position. Higher = stricter (fewer, safer matches); lower catches noisier re-encodes but risks false positives. The log records each match's similarity so you can calibrate.
MainWindow.Settings.PartialClipRequireVi: Confirm partial-clip matches by also checking video frames at the matched offset. Filters false positives from videos sharing an audio track but with different visuals (e.g. TikToks reusing a song). Adds a few frame decodes per match. Uses pHash if 'Use perceptual hashing' is enabled, else 32x32 grayscale percent diff.
MainWindow.Settings.PartialClipVisualThr: Minimum visual similarity at the matched offset for a partial-clip pair to be kept. Usually set slightly below the main visual threshold, since partial clips are often cropped or re-encoded.
DupList.AlreadyDeletedTooltip: This file was deleted while its drive was mounted. Its fingerprint is kept so a re-download of the same content is recognized.
Message.PruneGhostEntriesDone: {0:N0} ghost entries removed and the database saved.
DupList.OfflineTooltip: This file's drive is not currently connected.
Message.PruneGhostEntriesConfirm: {0:N0} entries have no comparable data and their files are gone from mounted drives. Remove them from the database?
MainWindow.Tooltip.PruneGhostEntries: Removes entries whose file is gone from a mounted drive AND that carry no comparable data (no frame hashes, no audio fingerprint). Tombstones — missing files whose fingerprints are kept to recognize re-downloads — are untouched, as are files on offline drives.
Message.NoGhostEntries: No ghost entries found: every missing file either has fingerprints (kept as a tombstone) or is on an offline drive.
MainWindow.Settings.RememberDeletedConte: Keep the fingerprint of files whose last copy was deleted, and include it in future comparisons: a re-download of content you already deleted is flagged 'Already deleted'. Database cleanup then preserves these tombstones (use Prune Ghost Entries for hygiene). Unplugged drives count as offline, never as deleted.
MainWindow.Settings.AutoCheckDeletedCont: After a scan, pre-check the live files in any group that matched deleted content (an 'Already deleted' entry), so they are ready for review and deletion. Nothing is ever deleted automatically — the delete button and its confirmation still decide.
Results.Hint.PreviewSize: Tip: previews too small? Drag the handle next to the Preview header to resize them — and raise the thumbnail width in the settings for sharper images.
Results.Header.PreviewResizeTip: Drag to resize — thumbnails grow with the column
Results.Action.Hint: Delete asks for confirmation and shows what gets freed.
Setup.Welcome.Title: New here?
Setup.Welcome.Text: Add the folders you want to check, pick what to look for, press Scan. That's the whole workflow.
Setup.NoDuplicates.Title: No duplicates found
Setup.NoDuplicates.Text: Your last scan finished and found no duplicate files.
Setup.LocationsFootnote: {0} locations · counts fill in as folders are indexed — scanning never waits for them.
Profile.Exact.Name: Exact & near duplicates
Profile.Exact.Desc: Copies, renames and re-encodes of the same video or photo.
Profile.Edited.Name: Edited & altered copies
Profile.Edited.Desc: Also finds crops, watermarks, flips and quality changes.
Profile.Ai.Name: AI scan
Profile.Ai.Desc: Also finds cropped, mirrored and re-edited copies, and clips cut from longer videos — without decoding any audio.
Profile.Deep.Name: Deep clean
Profile.Deep.Desc: Everything above, plus audio-fingerprint clip matching (the most accurate, but slowest).
Profile.Custom.Name: Custom
Profile.Custom.Desc: Your saved settings, untouched. For people who know the knobs.
Scan.Title: Scanning
Setup.Drop.Text: to include them — hold Alt to exclude · or
Settings.Banner.Managed1: These values are managed by the
Settings.Banner.Managed2: profile. Changing anything switches this scan setup to
Settings.Banner.Managed3: — nothing is lost, and you can return to the profile anytime.
Settings.Banner.Custom: These values are your own — no scan profile manages them. Selecting a profile on the main screen replaces them; picking Custom afterwards restores your values.
Settings.Row.Percent.Title: Similarity threshold
Settings.Row.Percent.Desc: How alike the sampled frames of two files must be to count as duplicates. 100 % finds only near-identical frames; ~95 % catches typical re-encodes; lower also surfaces watermarked or cropped copies at the cost of false positives.
Settings.Row.UsePHash.Title: Use perceptual hashing (pHash)
Settings.Row.UsePHash.Desc: Much faster comparison on large libraries and robust to small brightness shifts; slightly coarser than the raw grayscale diff. Images always use grayscale regardless. Ignored on old databases with 16×16 graybyte values.
Settings.Row.PHashSampleRatio.Title: Required matching samples
Settings.Row.PHashSampleRatio.Desc: Minimum percentage of the sampled frame positions that must individually pass the similarity threshold in pHash mode. Higher values reduce false positives caused by a single coincidental frame; 60 % is a balanced default.
Settings.Row.AiMatching.Title: AI matching (additional pass)
Settings.Row.AiMatching.Desc: Also compare videos with neural image embeddings (DINOv2). Finds transformed copies the classic methods miss — cropped, mirrored, zoomed or heavily edited versions — as an extra pass on top of the mode above; found pairs get an AI chip in the results. Needs the AI components below (~100 MB one-time download) and adds roughly 50 ms per file to a scan.
Settings.Row.AiPercent.Title: AI similarity threshold
Settings.Row.AiPercent.Desc: How similar the AI embeddings of two files must be (100% = identical content). 94% is a conservative default; lower it to ~92% to find more aggressively edited copies at a slightly higher false-positive risk.
Settings.Row.AiComponents.Title: AI components
Settings.Row.AiComponents.Desc: The AI pass needs the ONNX Runtime library and the embedding model (Apache-2.0 licensed, ~100 MB total). Both are downloaded once and stored next to VDF's database. Nothing is uploaded anywhere — all analysis runs on this computer.
Settings.Row.AiPartial.Title: Detect partial duplicates visually (AI)
Settings.Row.AiPartial.Desc: Finds trimmed cuts and clips inside longer recordings by matching AI keyframe embeddings with a consistent time offset. Unlike the audio-based detection above, this also works on silent, muted or re-dubbed videos. Uses the same AI components; keyframe sampling runs as its own scan phase.
Settings.Row.AiPartialHit.Title: AI frame hit threshold
Settings.Row.AiPartialHit.Desc: Minimum similarity for a single keyframe pair to count as a hit; at least 4 hits must agree on one time offset before two videos are paired. 89% is calibrated for re-encoded cuts; raise it if unrelated videos get paired.
Settings.AiComponents.RuntimeMissing: Runtime missing or outdated — download required
Message.AiComponentsMissingPrompt: AI matching is enabled, but its components (ONNX Runtime + model, ~100 MB) have not been downloaded yet. Download them now?
Message.AiDownloadDone:
AI components are ready.

Installed to: {0}
Results.AiMatchedTooltip: Found by the AI pass: the classic comparison did not consider these files duplicates, but their AI embeddings match.
Settings.Row.Flip.Title: Compare horizontally flipped
Settings.Row.Flip.Desc: Catches mirror-image re-uploads; matches are marked as flipped in the results. Cost: roughly doubles the comparison work.
Settings.Row.IgnoreBW.Title: Ignore black / white pixels
Settings.Row.IgnoreBW.Desc: Letterboxed or bordered copies match their clean originals. In rare cases this can cause false positives. Slightly slower.
Settings.Row.Thumbnails.Title: Frames sampled per video
Settings.Row.Thumbnails.Desc: Spread evenly over the timeline (1 = midpoint only, 3 = 25/50/75 %). More frames = fewer false matches, slower scan and a bigger database.
Settings.Row.Thumbnails.Warn: Changing this re-extracts every video on the next scan.
Settings.Row.DurationDiff.Title: Duration tolerance
Settings.Row.DurationDiff.Desc: Two videos are compared at all only when their durations differ by less than this percentage; the smaller of the two allowances wins. 0 disables the percent rule. Floor / cap in seconds available under “more”.
Settings.Row.DurationDiffMin.Title: Duration tolerance floor (seconds)
Settings.Row.DurationDiffMax.Title: Duration tolerance cap (seconds)
Settings.Row.MaxSampling.Title: Sampling window (seconds)
Settings.Row.Parallelism.Title: Parallel workers
Settings.Row.HddParallelism.Title: Parallel files per hard disk
Settings.Row.HddParallelism.Desc: How many files are read at once from a single spinning hard disk (or network share). A spinning disk wastes most of its time seeking when many files are read in parallel, so a low value — 2 is a good default — keeps it fast while SSDs still use all parallel workers. Wrongly detected drives can be pinned in the settings file (DriveTypeOverrides).
Settings.Row.MatchingParallelism.Title: Matching worker threads
Settings.Row.MatchingParallelism.Desc: CPU workers for the comparison phases (visual matching, partial-clip matching). Separate from the parallelism setting above, which governs how files are READ and is tuned to your storage. 0 = automatic: use most of the CPU while keeping one or two cores free so the app stays responsive.
Settings.Row.FolderMatch.Title: Folder match mode & depth
Settings.Row.IncludeSubDirectories.Desc: Also scan every subfolder of the folders on your list.
Settings.Row.IncludeImages.Desc: Scan photos and other images too, not just videos.
Settings.Row.IgnoreReadOnlyFolders.Desc: Skip folders that are marked read-only.
Settings.Row.HardwareAcceleration.Desc: Let FFmpeg use the GPU to decode video where possible. 'auto' picks a suitable method; 'none' is the safe choice if decoding fails or thumbnails look corrupted.
Settings.Row.CustomCommands.Title: Custom open commands
Settings.Row.CustomCommands.Desc: Your own commands for opening one or several files, e.g. 'nemo --tabs %f' — %f stands for the file(s).
Settings.Row.ScheduledScan.Desc: Start a scan automatically every day at the given time while the app is running.
Settings.Row.ScheduledScanTime.Title: Scheduled scan time
Settings.Row.NotifyScheduled.Desc: Send a desktop notification when a scheduled scan finishes.
Settings.Row.DarkMode.Title: Dark mode
Settings.Row.DarkMode.Desc: Use the dark theme. Takes effect immediately.
Settings.Row.UseMica.Title: Use Mica
Settings.Row.UseMica.Desc: Translucent window background on Windows 11. Requires a restart.
Settings.Row.ThumbnailDoubleClick.Desc: What double-clicking a preview image in the results does.
Settings.Row.FileSize.Title: Limit by file size (MB)
Settings.Row.FileSize.Desc: Only files inside this size range take part in the comparison.
Settings.Row.PathContains.Desc: Only files whose full path contains at least one of these entries are compared.
Settings.Row.PathNotContains.Desc: Files whose full path contains any of these entries are skipped.

Settings.Tags.Shortcuts: keyboard shortcut hotkey key binding gesture
